sessions.intent: Redesign Data identity model: cross-type wraps share state by reference; events become Lists; Variables.Set becomes dumb storage; variable.set always-types via GetType; migrate [VariableName] handlers to Data<string> and delete Legacy emission.

before.context: PLang.Generators has two emission paths — Data/this.cs (typed) and Legacy/this.cs (raw scalars + [VariableName]). ~25 handlers in modules/list, modules/loop, modules/variable still use Legacy. The original todo (Documentation/Runtime2/todos.md:61) framed this as just an attribute migration, but design discussion uncovered a deeper issue: As<T> always wraps — losing Properties identity, event subscribers, and memory pointer sharing — even when the source matches T. That violates 'everything is Data' principle.
before.principle: Every plang variable IS Data. Runtime/engine/emission never touches .Value — only modules unwrap. Cross-type wraps share state (Name + Properties + event lists) by reference; only Type and the converted .Value are distinct. Same-type access returns source as-is, no allocation. variable.set is the sole binding-mint site.
open_questions_resolved: Drop Data<T> as a class? No — strong typing is critical for LLM/builder.
Wrap on As<T>? Only when T differs from source's T. Same-type → source as-is.
Properties live-shared? Yes, ref-shared — typed views are live windows into the variable.
Events through cross-type wraps? Yes, must propagate live. Convert C# events to Lists.
Variables.Set semantics? Dumb storage. variable.set owns Data construction.
variable.set type inference? Always-typed via GetType + if-chain for common types, reflection fallback.
Conversion failure on cross-type? Attempt conversion, surface typed error on fail (existing __resolutionError path).
Replacement-survival of subscribers? Unconditional — alias prev's lists onto dv on every Set replacement.
before.scope: Single branch, sweeping change. ~30 files: Data/this.cs (rewrite), Variables/this.cs (rewrite), Debug/this.cs (5 lines), ~25 handler files, generator emission paths, attributes, tests. Phases 1-4 foundation + 5 surface migration.

sessions.intent: Translate architect v1 plan into a test contract: identity tests for As<T> (same-type fast path, variance, cross-type wrap with Properties/event-list aliasing), name propagation, plang-assignability (string-not-iterable rule), Variables.Set dumb storage with subscriber survival, variable.set type inference, IsPlangIterable single-source-of-truth, handler migration patterns A/B/C, and end-to-end PLang behavior coverage.

before.received: architect v1 plan at .bot/runtime2-data-share-state/architect/v1/plan.md (6 phases: events→Lists, As<T> rewrite + IsPlangAssignable, Variables.Set dumb storage, variable.set always-types, handler migration to 3 shapes, generator/Legacy delete).
before.scope_assumptions: C# tests live in PLang.Tests/App/{DataTests,VariablesTests,Modules}; PLang tests live in Tests/Modules/{Variable,Loop,List,Debug}. New test files where coverage doesn't exist yet (identity, name propagation, subscriber survival, plang assignability). New PLang tests for end-to-end debug subscriber survival + variable.set type inference.
before.risk: Architect plan is sweeping (~30 files). The contract here is what the coder must satisfy — under-specifying identity preservation will let regressions slip; over-specifying (e.g. exact one-allocation count) ties the implementation to a specific code shape and hurts maintainability. Aim: behavioral contracts via reference equality on Properties/event-lists/.Value, not internal counts.

actions.context: Test plan: 8 batches, ~60 C# tests + 4 PLang test goals, mapped 1:1 to architect's 6 phases. Anchored on reference equality of Properties / event lists / .Value to pin identity preservation.

Phase 1+2 contract — events as Lists; As<T> identity rules (same-type/variance/cross-type/plain-Data); name propagation across full-match/literal/partial/unset; string-not-iterable + AsEnumerable single source of truth. 30 tests stubbed Assert.Fail.

Phase 3 contract — Variables.Set dumb storage. Set(string, object?, Type?) overload dropped per user call. Aliasing of three event lists on replacement; Properties NOT aliased (architect default). Includes Remove_FiresOnDelete pin since today's Remove doesn't fire it.

Phase 4 contract — variable.set is sole binding-mint site. If-chain over hot types (string/int/long/double/bool/DateTime/list/dict). Snapshot-clone for mutable refs. Forced type via Type parameter with conversion failure path. Null arm. AsDefault preserved.
Phase 5 spot-checks — Pattern A (plain Data live ref) on list.add: handler return is live var Data, no Variables.Set write-back. Foreach over string runs body once, not per char.
Phase 6 contract — PLNG001 allowed-shape list shrinks: Data<T>, plain Data, [Provider] T pass; [VariableName] string now rejected; raw scalars still rejected.

PLang end-to-end coverage — type inference, snapshot clone on assign, list-add visibility across goal-call boundary (Pattern A), foreach over string runs once. Helper goals split per memory rule (one goal per file). TypeInference depends on coder adding assert.type action.
Session summary, root cross-version summary, verdict pass.

after.tests_added: 62 C# tests + 4 PLang .test.goal files (+ 2 helper goals)
after.build: PLang.Tests build clean (0 errors, pre-existing warnings only)
after.discovery: EventListTests sample run: 6/6 discovered, failing Assert.Fail as expected
open_decisions_made: Drop Set(string, object?, Type?) overload entirely (user call)
Properties NOT aliased on replacement (architect default kept)
PLang test goals use 'assert type of' syntax — coder adds the assert.type action
after.next: Coder reads architect/v1/plan.md (design) + test-designer/v1/plan.md (contract) and lands the implementation. All 62 C# tests + 4 PLang tests must pass.

sessions.intent: Implement architect/v1's 6-phase Data identity preservation + [VariableName] migration: events→Lists, As<T> identity-preserving wrap, name propagation, plang-assignability (string-not-iterable), Variables.Set dumb storage, variable.set always-types, handler migration to plain Data / Data<string> / Data<T>, Legacy emission delete.

before.baseline_action: Pulled origin/runtime2-data-share-state. Rebuilt plang. Ran plang --test from /Tests: 209 total → 145 pass, 56 fail, 8 stale. Renamed 64 failing/stale *.test.goal → *.test.goal2 to land a clean 145/145 baseline. Will bring them back online incrementally as each phase clears them.
before.scope: ~30 files: PLang/App/Data/this.cs (rewrite), PLang/App/Variables/this.cs (rewrite), PLang/App/Debug/this.cs (4 lines), ~25 handler files in App/modules/{variable,list,loop}/, generator emission + attributes, ~62 stub C# tests filled + 4 PLang test goals.
before.approach: Phased commits: 1+2 foundation, 3 Variables.Set, 4 variable.set, 5a foreach, 5b variable handlers, 5c list handlers, 6 generator+attribute deletion. Each phase ends with green C# + plang tests.

actions.context: Renamed 64 failing/stale .test.goal → .test.goal2 to land 145/145 baseline before sweeping changes. Brought back 21 of them (Modules/Loop/Foreach/Dictionary, Modules/Test/* + TestModule/* duplicates) as Phases 1+2 cleared them.

Phase 1+2: events→Lists, As<T> identity-preserving wrap (WrapAs/ConstructWrap), name propagation via canonical recursion, IsPlangIterable/IsPlangAssignable predicates, AsCanonical for plain-Data slots. Generator emits __ResolveData(...).AsCanonical(Context) for plain Data.

Phase 3: Variables.Set is dumb storage (Ingi: clone semantics live in variable.set, not in Variables.Set replacement). Variables.Remove fires OnDelete. Architect's 'alias prev events onto dv' idea dropped.

Phase 4: variable.set rewrite. MintTyped if-chain + reflection fallback for type inference. CarryStateFromSource (clone Properties + events from source Data per Ingi). SnapshotClone via JSON roundtrip with Data.UnwrapJsonElement to fix STJ JsonElement leak.
Filled in 53 test-designer stubs as real assertions. SubscriberSurvivalTests REWRITTEN per Ingi (no event-list aliasing on Variables.Set replacement; pins dumb-storage; Remove_FiresOnDelete pinned).

Plan + summaries documenting decisions (clone semantics, dumb Variables.Set), implementation (per-phase commits), deferred work (5b/5c/6 need .pr rebuild), and known issues (LLM build pipeline broken upstream, ContextVars.test references missing !engine system var).

after.status: Phases 1-4 complete + 5a spot-check tests; 5b/5c/6 deferred (need .pr rebuild)
after.notes: Build pipeline (LLM) broken upstream on this branch — couldn't add new PLang test goals. Deferred work documented in v1/summary.md. Suggested next: codeanalyzer for OBP review of Data.cs rewrite + variable.set.

sessions.intent: Review runtime2-data-share-state for OBP/simplification/readability and trace the Data lifecycle: where Data is created, where .Value is unwrapped (must only be in module.action), no Data-in-Data wrapping, no redundant copies/allocations beyond cross-type cast.

before.context: Branch coder/v1 landed phases 1-4 + 5a spot-check tests of architect/v1's 6-phase plan. Identity model for Data was rewritten: events as Lists, As<T> with same-type/variance/cross-type/error rules, AsCanonical for plain Data slots, Variables.Set as dumb storage, variable.set as sole binding-mint site with type inference + clone semantics. Phases 5b/5c/6 deferred (need .pr rebuild).
before.scan_targets: PLang/App/Data/this.cs (rewrite epicenter), PLang/App/Variables/this.cs, PLang/App/Debug/this.cs, PLang/App/modules/variable/set.cs (rewrite), PLang.Generators/Emission/Property/Data/this.cs, plus all module action handlers to trace .Value unwrap discipline.
before.ingis_extra_lens: Lifecycle audit — Data must be created lazily, never wrapped in Data, .Value used only at module.action; goal is zero-overhead pass-through. The only legitimate new Data() call is when casting an internal value to a different type.

actions.context: Read end-to-end the five rewrite files. Cross-checked AsT_Impl + WrapAs + AsCanonical + ConstructWrap call paths, the Variables.Set dumb-storage branch, variable.set MintTyped + CarryStateFromSource + SnapshotClone, the Debug List<Action> migration, and the plain-Data emission switch to AsCanonical.

Lifecycle audit per Ingi: grepped 79 `new Data.@this` sites and triaged each (variable.set mint, cross-type cast, error sentinel, navigator child, module value-producing boundary, etc). Grepped `.Value` reads in PLang/App/Goals, /Actor, PLang.Generators to verify unwrap discipline. Confirmed no Data-in-Data in production, copies match architect's 1-alloc-per-cross-cast contract except finding #2.

Plan, full per-file findings (5-pass review + lifecycle audit), version summary, root cross-version summary, verdict. Four concrete cleanups, none blocking: dead `if (!resolved.Success)` in AsCanonical (Data/this.cs:471-473), redundant transient Data alloc in WrapAs IEnumerable branch (:642-647), dead-but-mutating dv.Type branch in Variables.Set:71, three duplicated JSON-roundtrip clone blocks → extract Data.SnapshotClone. Architecture verdict CLEAN; review verdict NEEDS WORK pending the four cleanups.

after.health: build clean (0 errors); identity-model architecturally clean and lifecycle-faithful
after.findings: 1 dead code, 1 redundant alloc, 1 dead-mutating branch, 1 three-way duplication. All 5-minute fixes.
after.lifecycle_audit: PASS — Data creation discipline upheld (variable.set sole binding-mint site for variables); .Value unwrap stays in module.actions on the new path; no Data-in-Data in production; redundant copies = 1 (finding #2).
after.next: coder for the four findings, then tester to verify no drift in the 2524 C# tests.

sessions.intent: Verify coder/v1 review-response (commit 60b8d1f3) addressed the four codeanalyzer/v1 findings; re-run all five passes on the diff for fresh issues.

before.received: coder commit 60b8d1f3 'coder v1 review: address codeanalyzer/v1 findings' — 5 files / 38+/48− lines: AsCanonical dead conditional collapsed, WrapAs IEnumerable transient inlined, Variables.Set type mutation deleted, three JSON-roundtrip clones extracted to Data.SnapshotClone(object) + Json.SnapshotClone options.
v1_findings: Data.cs:642–647 transient Data alloc on cross-type IEnumerable wrap
Variables.cs:71 dead-but-side-effecting `dv.Type = type` — no caller passes type+Data
Three copies of JSON-roundtrip clone → Data.SnapshotClone helper
v1_subfindings_left_open: set.cs:117–118 defensive `?? new List<>` / `?? new Dict<>` on SnapshotClone result (cast can never produce null) — flagged as minor in v1
Data.cs same-type fast path comment nit
set.cs [VariableName] Pattern A pointer comment nit
before.risk: An extraction can silently change behavior at the absorbed call sites. The three OLD clone blocks were NOT identical — only set.cs had UnwrapJsonElement; the other two did not. Need to verify that lifting both Variables.cs and list/add.cs onto a UnwrapJsonElement-bearing helper is intended.

actions.context: Verified all four v1 findings closed by commit 60b8d1f3. Ran 5 passes on the diff; no new OBP/dead-code/redundant-alloc issues. Pass 4 caught a quiet behavior unification: extraction of Data.SnapshotClone added UnwrapJsonElement at two callsites that previously lacked it (Variables.cs dot-path, list/add.cs). New behavior is correct (no JsonElement leaks downstream); flagged for awareness, not as a fix request.
v2 deliverables — plan, v1 review summary, full result, verdict.json (pass), v2 summary, and bot-root summary update.

after.health: All four v1 findings fixed correctly. Helper extraction is sound. Two cosmetic carryovers remain, non-blocking.
after.notes: Pass 4 finding to flag: SnapshotClone extraction quietly unified divergent behavior — Variables.cs dot-path and list/add.cs previously omitted UnwrapJsonElement; new helper applies it everywhere. New behavior is correct (no JsonElement leaks). Commit message framed as pure dedup; result.md/summary.md document the unification. Suggested next: tester.

sessions.intent: Validate test quality for runtime2-data-share-state coder/v1 (after codeanalyzer/v2 CLEAN). Run full C#/PLang suites; coverage on touched files; deletion-test assertions; verify the 9 stub tests are honest stubs; check codeanalyzer/v2's behavioral concern (UnwrapJsonElement now applies at all 3 sites) has tests covering the now-Dictionary list-entry shape and Variables.Set dot-path.

before.received: coder/v1 + review-response 60b8d1f3 + codeanalyzer/v2 CLEAN at 8e0a419d. Coder claims 2524/2533 C# (9 forward-looking stubs), 166/166 plang. The behavioral concern from codeanalyzer/v2 — extraction of SnapshotClone unifies UnwrapJsonElement across three call sites — needs explicit test coverage check.
before.scope: Run tests, run coverage, scan all new/modified test files for false greens with deletion-test pass, verify deferred stubs are properly stubbed (not silently passing), check whether the now-Dictionary shape after SnapshotClone unification has any test that would have caught the JsonElement leak.
before.risk: Coder rewrote variable/set.cs and Data.As<T> identity model — ref-equality assertions are the only thing pinning that contract. Weak ref-equality wording (e.g. value equality instead of reference equality) would let a future regression slip through silently. Phase 5b/5c/6 stubs being silently passing instead of failing would also create false confidence.

actions.context: Tester plan: deletion-test + ref-equality discipline check on coder/v1 test files; coverage on 7 production files; verify 9 stubs honestly fail; trace codeanalyzer/v2 behavioral concern (UnwrapJsonElement now applies in Variables.Set + list.add) for test coverage.

Ran C# tests (2533 total, 2524 pass, 9 fail = exactly the deferred stubs). Ran coverage. High coverage on Data/this.cs (86%), Variables/this.cs (91%), variable/set.cs (89%). Lower on list/add.cs (56%) — entirely the new SnapshotClone path uncovered.

Read all 8 new test files + 1 test stubbed file. Applied deletion test mechanically. Confirmed ReferenceEquals discipline across AsTIdentityTests (10), PlangAssignabilityTests (8), SubscriberSurvivalTests (8). 9 stubs use Assert.Fail('Not implemented') — fail honestly. Pivot from 'alias prev events' to 'dumb storage' is correctly reflected.

Verdict: approved. 7 findings — 4 major (coverage gaps: MintTyped cold types, list.add complex-snapshot path, Variables.Set dot-path JsonElement shape, set.cs error paths) + 3 minor. No false greens — every aliasing claim uses ReferenceEquals. Recommend approve-with-deferred-coverage; auditor next.

sessions.intent: Investigate plang test failures, rebuild as needed; trace builder NRE to root cause; fix without hand-editing .pr.

before.builder_status: plang build crashes with NRE in OpenAiProvider.Query line 64 on every build
before.hypothesis_at_start: Stale .pr files in lowercase tests/modifiers/ (duplicates of Tests/Modules/Modifiers/). Builder NRE separate.

actions.context: Lowercase tests/ was byte-identical to Tests/Modules/Modifiers and Tests/Simple. User confirmed all tests live under uppercase Tests/. Stale scaffolder test was orphaned bot output.

Extracted IsWalkableContainer + WalkContainerVars helpers. AsCanonical now walks list/dict for nested %vars% the same way AsT_Impl does. Without this, plain Data slots (e.g. variable.set.Value) saw literal %var% strings inside parameter lists/dicts loaded from .pr.

Added JsonNode to complex-source dispatch (line ~336) and a parallel JsonArray element-iteration arm (mirrors existing JsonElement-array case). JsonObject implements IDictionary<string, JsonNode?> not IDictionary<string, object?> so it slipped past every dispatch arm before this fix; JsonArray doesnt implement non-generic IList so it skipped element iteration.

4 new tests for AsCanonical-with-nested-vars (list, dict, list-of-dicts, literal-list value preservation). 2 new tests for JsonObject->class and JsonArray->List<class> conversion. Tests close the gap that allowed the divergence between plain Data and Data<T> resolution to ship.

after.csharp_test_status: 2530/2539 pass (9 pre-existing Not-implemented placeholders unchanged; 6 new tests added all green)
after.builder_status: plang build Start.goal progresses past the original NRE, reaches LLM, fails later at separate pre-existing Actor-param validation (Cannot convert String to this) — out of scope for this fix.
after.notes: Two related bugs fixed: variable resolution asymmetry (plain vs typed Data) and JsonNode-to-typed conversion gap. Both root causes — not workarounds. WalkContainerVars is now single source of truth for nested-var walking.

sessions.intent: Review coder/v2 (commit 24cba238) for OBP/simplification/readability/behavioral correctness; user asked extra focus on the variable-resolution walk for the json→LlmMessage path.

before.received: coder commit 24cba238 'coder v2: nested %var% resolution in plain Data + JsonNode conversion'. Two production files (Data/this.cs +48/-15, TypeConverter.cs +22/-7) and two test files (6 new tests). Plus stale-tests/ and scaffolder cleanup (out of code review scope).
before.scope: PLang/App/Data/this.cs (WalkContainerVars + IsWalkableContainer extraction; AsCanonical container walk branch added; AsT_Impl reuses helper). PLang/App/Utils/TypeConverter.cs (JsonNode added to complex-source dispatch + parallel JsonArray element-iteration arm).
before.extra_focus: User asked for extra weight on Pass 4 — trace the var-walk through the json→LlmMessage path end to end. Specifically: are AsCanonical and AsT_Impl now truly symmetric for nested vars? Does the action-destination carve-out (AsT_Impl skips walk for Action template targets) need to apply to AsCanonical too? Cycle protection for the container path?
before.risk: An asymmetric fix could leave a path where plain Data still sees literal %vars%. The walk is recursive; cycle-protection is string-only on AsT_Impl side. The JsonArray arm could miss edge shapes (typed JsonValue, mixed JsonNode/object dicts). The carve-out for Action templates only exists in AsT_Impl — if a plain Data slot ever holds an action template, walking would resolve %vars% that the action template wants to keep raw.

actions.context: Five-pass review of coder/v2 with extra weight on Pass 4 (behavioral) per user request. Traced the var-walk end-to-end through the json→LlmMessage path: .pr parse → AsCanonical container branch → WalkContainerVars → resolved list → TryConvertTo(list, JsonNode) → JsonArray storage → downstream As<List<LlmMessage>> → new JsonArray arm → recursive TryConvertTo(JsonNode-element, LlmMessage) → typed LlmMessage. Symmetry between AsCanonical and AsT_Impl verified for the container path. Action-template carve-out works at the per-element level via SubstitutePrimitive's guards. Cycle protection unaffected (container walk is single-level).

Cross-checks: confirmed the generator-emitted plain-Data path goes through AsCanonical(Context); enumerated all `partial Data.@this` slots across modules to confirm no plain-Data slot legitimately holds an action template (so AsCanonical does not need a parallel to AsT_Impl's IsActionDestination carve-out); confirmed Variables.Resolve is single-pass (no recursive %var% expansion at the container-element level); confirmed TypeMapping.GetType("json") → typeof(JsonNode) so `set Type=json` is the path that mints Data<JsonNode>.

v3 deliverables — plan, recap of v2 PASS state, full result with end-to-end var-walk trace and per-file findings, verdict.json (pass), v3 summary, and bot-root summary update. Three findings (1 simplification, 1 informational on TypeConverter, 1 durable test gap on container-walk aliasing); all cosmetic-or-add-only, none blocking.

after.health: Both fixes correct and minimal. Symmetry between AsCanonical and AsT_Impl holds for the container path. JsonNode dispatch covers JsonObject/JsonArray/JsonValue. Test coverage on the new bug surfaces is solid (4+2 tests).
after.findings: 1 simplification (transient-builder dedup in AsCanonical), 1 readability (IsWalkableContainer + WalkContainerVars shape mirror), 1 informational (JsonArray arm silently drops per-element errors, matching JsonElement arm but diverging from IList arm), 1 test gap (no test pins state-aliasing on the container-walk transient — one ReferenceEquals assertion would close it).
after.extra_focus_passed: User-requested Pass 4 deep dive on the json→LlmMessage path: 10-stage end-to-end trace passed; symmetry verified; carve-outs intact.
after.carryovers_from_prior_versions: set.cs:117-118 unreachable `??` fallbacks and global:: prefix in 3 callsites — out of scope for this commit, still present.
after.next: tester — verify the 6 new tests pass green, optionally add the missing aliasing test, re-run the LLM-builder smoke path now that Start.goal builds past the NRE. If pass → auditor → merge.

sessions.intent: Validate test quality for runtime2-data-share-state coder/v2 (after codeanalyzer/v3 PASS). Run full suites, coverage on the 2 production files, deletion test on the 6 new tests, and verify codeanalyzer/v3's flagged test gap (state-aliasing on AsCanonical container-walk transient).

before.received: coder/v2 commit 24cba238 + codeanalyzer/v3 PASS at ae827527. The new tests assert on resolved values + ReferenceEquals(canonical, paramData)==false but may not pin the four state-aliasing lines. If true, that's a major false-green on the architect's identity-share-by-reference contract for plain Data slots.

actions.context: Tester plan: deletion-test on 6 new tests, coverage on 2 production files, verify codeanalyzer/v3's predicted gap, status of v1 carryovers.

Read all 6 new tests. Ran C# (2530/2539, 9 deferred stubs) and plang (166/166). Mechanical deletion test on each new production line: 5 of 6 pin. Codeanalyzer/v3's flagged gap CONFIRMED — removing all 4 state-aliasing lines on Data/this.cs:491-494 yields 14/14 green (false-green). Same shape on partial-interp branch L476-479 (pre-existing). Rule 4f (LiteralList) does not pin the walk.
v2 deliverables — plan, full result with deletion-test table, summary, verdict (pass), root tester summary updated. 1 major + 2 minor + 1 informational findings.

after.coverage_summary: 100% line-coverage on AsCanonical container branch, JsonArray arm, JsonNode dispatch. Data/this.cs 87.4%, TypeConverter.cs 57.8% (large pre-existing untouched paths).
after.findings_count: 1 major + 2 minor + 1 informational. Codeanalyzer/v3's predicted state-aliasing gap CONFIRMED via deletion test.
after.key_strengths: Coder/v2's 5 of 6 new production changes are properly pinned via deletion test. New code is 100% line-covered. Rules 4c/4d/4e all genuinely fail when the walk is removed.
after.key_gaps: 1 major false-green: state-aliasing on AsCanonical container-walk transient (L491-494) not asserted — 4 lines can be deleted with all 14 tests green. Pre-existing same shape on partial-interp branch (L476-479). Rule 4f LiteralList doesn't actually pin the walk (values pass through identically).
after.v1_carryovers: 6 of 7 open. Closed: #7 stale tests/modifiers/ (deleted by coder/v2 cleanup). Still open: #1 MintTyped cold types, #2 list.add complex-snapshot, #3 Variables.Set dot-path JsonElement, #4 Set error paths, #5 Set_IntValue permissive, #6 Replacement-NOT-fires-OnDelete.

sessions.intent: Audit runtime2-data-share-state for cross-file contract gaps the four prior reviewers (architect, codeanalyzer x3, tester x2) may have missed; verify foundation ripple from Phase 1+2 events->Lists + Phase 3 dumb-storage flip.

before.assumptions: Codeanalyzer/v1-v3 + tester/v1-v2 all approved. Dumb-storage Variables.Set is the user-confirmed design. Architect/v1 plan Phase 3 has an open question (line 290) about whether event lists alias from prev to dv on replacement; Ingi confirmed dumb storage (no alias). C# tests: 2530/2539, plang tests: 166/166.
before.risk: The flip from architect-original (alias prev events) to dumb-storage (no alias) changed observable behavior. Whoever depends on subscriber-survival-on-replacement is silently broken if not updated. Debug/this.cs:141-160 implements the placeholder-subscribe pattern that depends on exactly this. No test covers the flow end-to-end.

actions.context: Wrote plan: scope-of-branch matrix, what each prior reviewer covered, what no reviewer traced (cross-file Debug ↔ Variables.Set seam), and the audit approach.

Wrote and ran a focused TUnit test mirroring App/Debug/this.cs:145-159 placeholder-subscribe pattern through Variables.Set + a manual CarryStateFromSource shape. Test confirms the regression: two consecutive sets on a watched variable fire OnChange exactly once. Test passes (asserting calls==1) - the broken behavior is the current behavior. File deleted after verification (production fix is the coder s job).

Wrote auditor findings: 1 major (cross-file Debug seam regression), 1 minor (tester/v2 inherited false-green), 2 nits (cosmetic carryovers). Verdict: FAIL -> suggest coder. Each finding cites file:line, missed_by, impact, suggested fix.

Read all 8 production files in scope. Spot-checked As<T> four-rule contract, AsCanonical symmetry with typed walk, cycle protection (HashSet + ResolveDepthLimit), dumb-storage Variables.Set, MintTyped if-chain, JsonArray + JsonNode dispatch in TypeConverter, list.add snapshot path with catch fallback. All clean within their own scope.

Read all prior reviews end-to-end. Architect raised the dumb-storage contract question on plan.md:290 -> Ingi flipped to dumb storage; no reviewer ran the inverse trace (who depends on subscriber-survival across replacement). That gap is what F1 captures.

after.health: audit complete; coder receives back with 1 major + 1 minor + 2 nits
after.notes: Build clean, C# 2530/2539 (9 honest deferred stubs), plang 166/166 - suite is honest. Per-file work is sound. Cross-file regression: Debug/this.cs placeholder-subscribe pattern silently broken under new dumb-storage Variables.Set. Verified empirically. Architect raised it as an open question, Ingi flipped the contract, Debug was not updated to match. The fix is an Ingi-level architectural call (restore subscriber-survival on dv replacement, or move Debug to a Variables-collection-level subscription API).
